title: Add a causal short-history sequence sidecar and use it only as an ensemble component
summary: Public F1 pit-stop studies report that Bi-LSTM/TCN-style models outperform weaker sequence baselines because pit calls depend on recent temporal trends in tyre wear, lap-time drift, and stint evolution.
rationale: Public F1 pit-stop studies report that Bi-LSTM/TCN-style models outperform weaker sequence baselines because pit calls depend on recent temporal trends in tyre wear, lap-time drift, and stint evolution. That pattern is missing from the current row-wise feature pipeline.
implementation_hint: Create fixed-length windows from the last 5-10 laps for each `Race-Driver` using only past observations, with masking for short histories. Train a compact GRU/BiLSTM/TCN sidecar and keep the existing tree model as the anchor; stack or average OOF predictions rather than betting on the sequence model alone.
expected_effect: Should recover local trend information around pit windows that static engineered features only approximate.
risk: Sequence models are more brittle on tabular sports data and may overfit if the split is not race-purged or if bidirectional context accidentally includes future laps.
